rows.definition: The full drive record for one served run (run-state, budget, priority, pace), keyed by TraceID and persisting across turns.
rows.distinction: The persistent RECORD across turns - NOT a Turn (one round-trip within it) and NOT a Slot (the free/busy signal).

rows.definition: One model round-trip within a session: the agent submits input, the model generates output, and results are admitted to context.
rows.distinction: ONE round-trip - NOT a Session (which spans many turns) and NOT a Slot (the scheduling event at a turn boundary).

rows.definition: The immutable free/busy signal emitted when a session leaves the eligible set (budget exhaustion, pause, drain, stop), freeing scheduling capacity.
rows.distinction: The free/busy SIGNAL at turn boundaries - NOT the Session (persistent state) and NOT the Budget (the allotment whose exhaustion fires it).

rows.definition: The policy layer that reads a Table's Snapshot and selects the live session that should run next (StrictPriority or WeightedFair).
rows.distinction: The POLICY that picks a winner - NOT the Table (the mutable store) and NOT a Session (one state record).

rows.definition: A session's remaining work allotment across three independent axes: turns (round-trips), output tokens, and context tokens.
rows.distinction: TOTAL remaining work - NOT Pace (the per-turn throttle) and NOT a Slot (the signal fired when budget is exhausted).
gaps: budget/pace/runstate are defined-only, not yet in the glossary

rows.definition: The per-turn throttle: max output tokens and minimum gap between turns, applied cooperatively without pausing the session.
rows.distinction: PER-TURN throttle (how hard one turn runs) - NOT Budget (total remaining work across all turns).
gaps: budget/pace/runstate are defined-only, not yet in the glossary

rows.definition: A served session's lifecycle position in a small total state machine: Running, Throttled, Paused, Draining, or Stopped.
rows.distinction: ONE field carrying the session's phase - NOT the Session (the full record that contains it).
gaps: budget/pace/runstate are defined-only, not yet in the glossary
